16S SOD Experiments

2024-05-21

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/Documents/Postdoc/SOD_Microbiome")
library(phyloseq); packageVersion("phyloseq")
## [1] '1.50.0'
library(ggplot2); packageVersion("ggplot2")
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.4.3
## [1] '4.0.2'
library(readr); packageVersion("readr")
## [1] '2.1.5'
library(purrr); packageVersion("purrr")
## [1] '1.2.0'

## The following object is masked from 'package:cowplot':

## [1] '0.6.1'
theme_set(theme_cowplot())
library(geosphere); packageVersion("geosphere")
## [1] '1.5.20'

Load data and filter out wildfire samples

load("Data/16S/filtered_data/sodexp.RData")
load("Data/16S/filtered_data/sod_5asv.RData")

filtered_read_counts<-read.csv("Data/16S/filtered_data/16S_subset_track_reads_sod.csv")
summary(filtered_read_counts)
##       X                 input          filtered       denoisedF    
##  Length:98          Min.   :16297   Min.   :12561   Min.   :11069  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:28042   1st Qu.:22272   1st Qu.:19990  
##  Mode  :character   Median :34454   Median :26962   Median :24454  
##                     Mean   :36682   Mean   :28478   Mean   :26069  
##                     3rd Qu.:42840   3rd Qu.:33323   3rd Qu.:30554  
##                     Max.   :91755   Max.   :70974   Max.   :65545  
##    denoisedR         merged         nonchim     
##  Min.   :10734   Min.   : 8050   Min.   : 8029  
##  1st Qu.:19866   1st Qu.:15877   1st Qu.:15635  
##  Median :24366   Median :20056   Median :19814  
##  Mean   :25988   Mean   :21519   Mean   :21114  
##  3rd Qu.:30522   3rd Qu.:25226   3rd Qu.:25044  
##  Max.   :65421   Max.   :55313   Max.   :54662
new_names <- sapply(strsplit(sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_name16S, "-"), function(x) {
  substr(x[1], 3, nchar(x[1]))
})

sod.ps@sam_data$Sample_name16S <- new_names
sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_name16S <- new_names

sod_nowildfire.ps <- subset_samples(sod.ps, Fire_Mod != "wildfire")
sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps <- subset_samples(sod_5asv.ps, Fire_Mod != "wildfire")

save(sod_nowildfire.ps, file="Data/16S/filtered_data/sodexp_nowildfire.RData")
save(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps, file="Data/16S/filtered_data/sod_5asv_nowildfire.RData")


sod_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP <- factor(sod_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP, 
                                            levels = c("untreated", "treated"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated", "Treated"))

sod_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR <- factor(sod_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR, 
                                            levels = c("untreated", "1to2", "3to4", "5to8", "10to12"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated", "1 to 2", "3 to 4", "5 to 8", "10 to 12"))

sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP <- factor(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP, 
                                            levels = c("untreated", "treated"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated", "Treated"))

sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR <- factor(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR, 
                                            levels = c("untreated", "1to2", "3to4", "5to8", "10to12"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated", "1 to 2", "3 to 4", "5 to 8", "10 to 12"))

Bar plot agglomerated by species-using filtered dataset and facet wrapped by treatment

data16S <- sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>%   
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Phylum ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(data16S, aes(x = Sample_name16S, y = Abundance, fill = Phylum)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option= "H", discrete = TRUE) +
  scale_color_viridis(option="H", discrete = TRUE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Phylum"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Treatuntreat_EP), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )
## Warning in geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_name16S", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

ggsave("Figures/16S/relabundance_plot_treatment_relabund_phylum_sod_nowildfire.pdf", abund_plot, width = 24, height = 10)

Make a similar plot, but color by order instead

data16S <- sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Order") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(data16S, aes(x = Sample_name16S, y = Abundance, fill = Order)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option= "H", discrete = TRUE) +
  scale_color_viridis(option="H", discrete = TRUE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Order"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Treatuntreat_EP), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )
## Warning in geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_name16S", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

ggsave("Figures/16S/relabundance_plot_treatment_relabund_order_sod_nowildfire.pdf", abund_plot, width = 24, height =16)

Let’s summarize with boxplot-treatment

data16S <- sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  filter(Abundance > 0.05)

combined_plot <- ggplot(data16S, aes(x = Treatuntreat_EP, y = Abundance, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +  # Boxplot with no outliers and some transparency
  geom_jitter(color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 2) +  # Jittered black dots
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option="H") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.position = "none",  # Remove the legend
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Phylum, scales = "free_y") +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 40)

ggsave("Figures/16S/relabundance_plot_treatment_combined_facet_boxplot_phylum_nowildfire.pdf", combined_plot, width = 30, height = 16)

Bar plot agglomerated by order-using filtered dataset and faceted by yr since trt

data16S <- sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Order") %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(data16S, aes(x = Sample_name16S, y = Abundance, fill = Order)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option= "H", discrete = TRUE) +
  scale_color_viridis(option="H", discrete = TRUE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Order"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(YrSinceTrt_BR), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )
## Warning in geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_name16S", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

ggsave("Figures/16S/relabundance_plot_yrsincetrt_relabund_order_sod_nowildfire.pdf", abund_plot, width = 30, height = 16)

Let’s summarize with boxplot-year since trt

data16S <- sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  filter(Abundance > 0.05)

combined_plot <- ggplot(data16S, aes(x = YrSinceTrt_BR, y = Abundance, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +  # Boxplot with no outliers and some transparency
  geom_jitter(color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 2) +  # Jittered black dots
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option="H") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.position = "none",  # Remove the legend
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Phylum, scales = "free_y") +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 40)

ggsave("Figures/16S/relabundance_plot_yrsincetrt_combined_facet_boxplot_phylum_nowildfire.pdf", combined_plot, width = 30, height = 16)

Let’s look top taxa at order level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Order")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/16S/top40_genus_sod_nowildfire.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_name16S", fill="Order") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the phyloseq package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/joey711/phyloseq/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s look top taxa at family level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Family")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/16S/top40_family_sod_nowildfire.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_name16S", fill="Family") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s look top taxa at genus level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Genus")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/16S/top40_genus_sod_nowildfire.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_name16S", fill="Genus") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2
data_raw <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Phylum") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_raw <- data_raw %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum) %>%
  summarise(Raw_Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
data_rel <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Phylum") %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)}) %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_rel <- data_rel %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100 * (Relative_Abundance / sum(Relative_Abundance))) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
data_summed <- data_summed_raw %>%
  left_join(data_summed_rel, by = c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Kingdom", "Phylum")) %>%
  arrange(Treatuntreat_EP, desc(Percentage))  # Sort by treatment, then percentage descending
  
write.csv(data_summed, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_phylum_treatment_nowildfire.csv", row.names = TRUE)

data_raw1 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_raw1 <- data_raw1 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Raw_Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
data_rel1 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)}) %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_rel1 <- data_rel1 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100 * (Relative_Abundance / sum(Relative_Abundance))) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
data_summed1 <- data_summed_raw1 %>%
  left_join(data_summed_rel1, by = c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus")) %>%
  arrange(Treatuntreat_EP, desc(Percentage))  # Sort
  
write.csv(data_summed1, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_genus_treatment_nowildfire.csv", row.names = TRUE)

data_raw2 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_raw2 <- data_raw2 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Raw_Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
data_rel2 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)}) %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_rel2 <- data_rel2 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Treatuntreat_EP) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100 * (Relative_Abundance / sum(Relative_Abundance))) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Treatuntreat_EP', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
data_summed2 <- data_summed_raw2 %>%
  left_join(data_summed_rel2, by = c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species")) %>%
  arrange(Treatuntreat_EP, desc(Percentage))  # Sort
  
write.csv(data_summed2, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_species_treatment_nowildfire.csv", row.names = TRUE)

Let’s make a table of top taxa-faceted by Years Since SOD treatment

data_raw3 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Phylum") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_raw3 <- data_raw3 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR, Kingdom, Phylum) %>%
  summarise(Raw_Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'YrSinceTrt_BR', 'Kingdom'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
data_rel3 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Phylum") %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)}) %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_rel3 <- data_rel3 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR, Kingdom, Phylum) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100 * (Relative_Abundance / sum(Relative_Abundance))) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'YrSinceTrt_BR', 'Kingdom'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
data_summed3 <- data_summed_raw3 %>%
  left_join(data_summed_rel3, by = c("YrSinceTrt_BR", "Kingdom", "Phylum")) %>%
  arrange(YrSinceTrt_BR, desc(Percentage))  # Sort by years, then percentage descending
  
write.csv(data_summed3, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_phylum_yrsincetrt_nowildfire.csv", row.names = TRUE)

data_raw4 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_raw4 <- data_raw4 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Raw_Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'YrSinceTrt_BR', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
data_rel4 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)}) %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_rel4 <- data_rel4 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100 * (Relative_Abundance / sum(Relative_Abundance))) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'YrSinceTrt_BR', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
data_summed4 <- data_summed_raw4 %>%
  left_join(data_summed_rel4, by = c("YrSinceTrt_BR", "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus")) %>%
  arrange(YrSinceTrt_BR, desc(Percentage))  # Sort
  
write.csv(data_summed4, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_genus_yrsincetrt_nowildfire.csv", row.names = TRUE)

data_raw5 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_raw5 <- data_raw5 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Raw_Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'YrSinceTrt_BR', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',
## 'Class', 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
data_rel5 <-
  sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)}) %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed_rel5 <- data_rel5 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(YrSinceTrt_BR) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100 * (Relative_Abundance / sum(Relative_Abundance))) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'YrSinceTrt_BR', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum',

data_summed5 <- data_summed_raw5 %>%
  left_join(data_summed_rel5, by = c("YrSinceTrt_BR", "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species")) %>%
  arrange(YrSinceTrt_BR, desc(Percentage))  # Sort
  
write.csv(data_summed5, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_sod_species_yrsincetrt_nowildfire.csv", row.names = TRUE)

Let’s look at spacial placement of different plots

pdf("Figures/16S/location_of_plots_nowildfire.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)
map_plot <- ggplot(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps@sam_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  geom_text(aes(color = Fire_Mod, label = Sample_name16S), size = 2.5, 
            check_overlap = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", title = "Sampling Locations") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20), 
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
print(map_plot)
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s normalize our data in preparation for beta diversity analyses

#sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps<- phyloseq::prune_taxa(taxa_sums(sod_nowildfire.ps) > 5, sod_nowildfire.ps)
sdt = data.table::data.table(as(sample_data(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps), "data.frame"),
                 TotalReads = sample_sums(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps), keep.rownames = TRUE)
min_reads<-min(sdt$TotalReads)

rarefied.ps <- rarefy_even_depth(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps, sample.size = min_reads, rngseed = 100)
## `set.seed(100)` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.
## Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.
## Try `set.seed(100); .Random.seed` for the full vector
## ...
## 1107OTUs were removed because they are no longer 
## present in any sample after random subsampling
## ...
strat_rel.ps<-transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv_nowildfire.ps, function(x) x / sum(x))

Alpha diversity-use non-normalized or pruned data

Let’s start with t-test as point of comparison with anova analysis

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod_nowildfire.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_nowildfire.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP")))

factors <- c("Treatuntreat_EP")
ttest_list <- list()

for (factor_name in factors) {
  ttest <- data.table(alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric())
  
  for (alphad in c("InvSimpson", "Shannon", "Observed")) {
    # Perform t-test
    ttest_res <- t.test(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad], var.equal = TRUE)
    p_value <- ttest_res$p.value
    ttest <- rbind(ttest, data.table(alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value))
  }
  
  ttest_list[[factor_name]] <- ttest
}

for (factor_name in factors) {
  cat(paste("T-Test Results for Factor:", factor_name), "\n")
  print(knitr::kable(ttest_list[[factor_name]], format = "markdown"))
  cat("\n")
}
## T-Test Results for Factor: Treatuntreat_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.0016273|
## |Shannon     | 0.0004055|
## |Observed    | 0.0001758|

Let’s now proceed with ANOVA analyses and then compile our alpha diversity stats

set.seed(1)
anova_list <- list()

AD <- estimate_richness(sod_nowildfire.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_nowildfire.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")))

factors2 <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")

plotted_factors <- character()

final_anova_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())

for (factor_name in factors2) {
  anova <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())
  
  for (alphad in c("Observed","InvSimpson", "Shannon")) {
    anova_res <- aov(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad])
    
    anova_summary <- summary(anova_res)
    
    p_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    
    df_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[1]
    df_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[2]
    ss_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[1]
    ss_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[2]
    ms_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[1]
    ms_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[2]
    F_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$"F value"[1]
    anova <- rbind(anova, data.table(factor_name = factor_name, alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value, df_between = df_between, df_within = df_within, ss_between = ss_between, ss_within = ss_within, ms_between = ms_between, ms_within = ms_within, F_value = F_value))
  }
  
  final_anova_table <- rbind(final_anova_table, anova)
}

print(knitr::kable(final_anova_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name      |alpha_index |    pvalue| df_between| df_within|   ss_between|    ss_within|   ms_between|    ms_within|   F_value|
## |:----------------|:-----------|---------:|----------:|---------:|------------:|------------:|------------:|------------:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP  |Observed    | 0.0001758|          1|        82| 1.092518e+05| 5.796909e+05| 1.092518e+05| 7069.4017511| 15.454173|
## |Treatuntreat_EP  |InvSimpson  | 0.0016273|          1|        82| 1.034800e+04| 7.988077e+04| 1.034800e+04|  974.1557727| 10.622527|
## |Treatuntreat_EP  |Shannon     | 0.0004055|          1|        82| 9.946198e-01| 5.997380e+00| 9.946198e-01|    0.0731388| 13.599075|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    | 0.0000851|          4|        79| 1.777468e+05| 5.111959e+05| 4.443671e+04| 6470.8337553|  6.867231|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  | 0.0004727|          4|        79| 2.007062e+04| 7.015815e+04| 5.017655e+03|  888.0778407|  5.650017|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     | 0.0001746|          4|        79| 1.701564e+00| 5.290435e+00| 4.253911e-01|    0.0669675|  6.352199|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Observed    | 0.0007039|          2|        81| 1.130461e+05| 5.758967e+05| 5.652303e+04| 7109.8352146|  7.949977|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |InvSimpson  | 0.0014585|          2|        81| 1.343642e+04| 7.679235e+04| 6.718211e+03|  948.0536659|  7.086320|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Shannon     | 0.0009298|          2|        81| 1.106996e+00| 5.885004e+00| 5.534978e-01|    0.0726544|  7.618231|
write.csv(final_anova_table, "Results/16S/final_anova_table_sod_nowildfire.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s make faceted boxplots for our alpha diversity analyses

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod_nowildfire.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_nowildfire.ps)[rownames(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")])

variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")

plots <- list()

for (variable in variables) {
  p <- plot_richness(sod_nowildfire.ps, x = variable, measures = c("Shannon", "InvSimpson", "Observed")) + 
    geom_boxplot()
  plots[[variable]] <- p
}

plots_arrange <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  plots[["Treatuntreat_EP"]], plots[["YrSinceTrt_BR"]],plots[["YrSinceTrt_Range"]],ncol=1)

plots_arrange_with_text <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_plot(plots_arrange) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Treatuntreat_EP", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("YrSinceTrt_BR", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("YrSinceTrt_Range", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) 
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
ggsave(plots_arrange_with_text, path = "Figures/16S", filename = "alpha_diversity_each_factor_sod_nowildfire.pdf", 
       width = 16, height = 24)

Let’s perform TUKEY HSD tests

set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

tukey_results <- list()

for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~ ."))
    anova_res <- aov(formula, data = data_subset)
    tukey_result <- HSD.test(anova_res, variable)
    print(tukey_result)
  }
}
## $statistics
##    MSerror Df     Mean       CV
##   7069.402 82 317.4405 26.48677
## 
## $parameters
##    test          name.t ntr StudentizedRange alpha

## 
## $means
##           Observed       std  r       se Min Max Q25 Q50 Q75

## Untreated 283.8667  51.23946 45 12.53387 207 385 237 276 326
## 
## $comparison
## NULL
## 
## $groups
##           Observed groups
## Treated   356.1795      a
## Untreated 283.8667      b
##

Let’s also perform Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn’s Post-hoc tests in case our data violates key ANOVA assumptions

set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

final_kw_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                              chi_squared = numeric(), df = numeric(), pvalue = numeric())

final_dunn_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                                Comparison = character(), Z = numeric(),
                                P.unadj = numeric(), P.adj = numeric())

for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~", variable))

    cat("\n=====", measure, "by", variable, "=====\n")
    kw_res <- kruskal.test(formula, data = data_subset)
    print(kw_res)

    final_kw_table <- rbind(final_kw_table, data.table(
      factor_name = variable, alpha_index = measure,
      chi_squared = unname(kw_res$statistic), df = unname(kw_res$parameter),
      pvalue = kw_res$p.value
    ))

    n_groups <- nlevels(droplevels(data_subset[[variable]]))
    if (n_groups == 2) {
      cat("(2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)\n")
    } else {
      dunn_res <- FSA::dunnTest(formula, data = data_subset, method = "bh")
      print(dunn_res)

      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_res$res
      dunn_table_sub$factor_name <- variable
      dunn_table_sub$alpha_index <- measure
      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_table_sub[, c("factor_name", "alpha_index", "Comparison", "Z", "P.unadj", "P.adj")]

      final_dunn_table <- rbind(final_dunn_table, dunn_table_sub)
    }
  }
}
## 
## ===== Observed by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 10.836, df = 1, p-value = 0.0009953
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 9.6581, df = 1, p-value = 0.001885
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 9.2718, df = 1, p-value = 0.002327
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Observed by YrSinceTrt_BR =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by YrSinceTrt_BR
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 15.782, df = 4, p-value = 0.003326
## Registered S3 methods overwritten by 'FSA':
##   method       from
##   confint.boot car 
##   hist.boot    car
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 15.7821, df = 4, p-value = 0
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │     1 to 2   10 to 12     3 to 4     5 to 8
## ─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────
## 10 to 12 │   1.520938
##          │     0.2566 
##          │
##   3 to 4 │  -0.323870  -1.340938
##          │     0.8289     0.2999 
##          │
##   5 to 8 │  -1.142940  -2.175047  -0.513948
##          │     0.3615     0.0988     0.7591 
##          │
## Untreate │   2.768585   0.167058   1.740977   3.028523
##          │     0.0282*    0.8673     0.2042     0.0246*
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##              Comparison          Z     P.unadj      P.adj
## 1     1 to 2 - 10 to 12  1.5209382 0.128275342 0.25655068
## 2       1 to 2 - 3 to 4 -0.3238704 0.746036175 0.82892908
## 3     10 to 12 - 3 to 4 -1.3409384 0.179940448 0.29990075
## 4       1 to 2 - 5 to 8 -1.1429403 0.253063364 0.36151909
## 5     10 to 12 - 5 to 8 -2.1750477 0.029626540 0.09875513
## 6       3 to 4 - 5 to 8 -0.5139485 0.607287982 0.75910998
## 7    1 to 2 - Untreated  2.7685852 0.005630026 0.02815013

print(knitr::kable(final_kw_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name      |alpha_index | chi_squared| df|    pvalue|
## |:----------------|:-----------|-----------:|--:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP  |Observed    |   10.836247|  1| 0.0009953|
## |Treatuntreat_EP  |InvSimpson  |    9.658100|  1| 0.0018852|
## |Treatuntreat_EP  |Shannon     |    9.271815|  1| 0.0023271|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |   15.782058|  4| 0.0033260|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |   14.845282|  4| 0.0050331|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |   15.337084|  4| 0.0040508|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Observed    |   10.842252|  2| 0.0044222|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |InvSimpson  |   10.688259|  2| 0.0047761|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Shannon     |    9.728294|  2| 0.0077184|
write.csv(final_kw_table, "Results/16S/final_kruskalwallis_table_sod_nowildfire.csv", row.names = FALSE)

print(knitr::kable(final_dunn_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name      |alpha_index |Comparison           |          Z|   P.unadj|     P.adj|
## |:----------------|:-----------|:--------------------|----------:|---------:|---------:|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |1 to 2 - 10 to 12    |  1.5209382| 0.1282753| 0.2565507|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |1 to 2 - 3 to 4      | -0.3238704| 0.7460362| 0.8289291|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |10 to 12 - 3 to 4    | -1.3409384| 0.1799404| 0.2999007|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |1 to 2 - 5 to 8      | -1.1429403| 0.2530634| 0.3615191|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |10 to 12 - 5 to 8    | -2.1750477| 0.0296265| 0.0987551|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |3 to 4 - 5 to 8      | -0.5139485| 0.6072880| 0.7591100|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |1 to 2 - Untreated   |  2.7685852| 0.0056300| 0.0281501|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |10 to 12 - Untreated |  0.1670589| 0.8673237| 0.8673237|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |3 to 4 - Untreated   |  1.7409775| 0.0816875| 0.2042188|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Observed    |5 to 8 - Untreated   |  3.0285235| 0.0024575| 0.0245752|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |1 to 2 - 10 to 12    |  0.4830873| 0.6290337| 0.6290337|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |1 to 2 - 3 to 4      | -1.9001434| 0.0574143| 0.1148286|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |10 to 12 - 3 to 4    | -1.9902414| 0.0465644| 0.1164109|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |1 to 2 - 5 to 8      | -1.0466892| 0.2952430| 0.4217757|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |10 to 12 - 5 to 8    | -1.2490573| 0.2116441| 0.3527402|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |3 to 4 - 5 to 8      |  0.9250418| 0.3549442| 0.4436802|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |1 to 2 - Untreated   |  2.0033455| 0.0451402| 0.1504674|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |10 to 12 - Untreated |  0.7841443| 0.4329555| 0.4810616|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |3 to 4 - Untreated   |  3.0015328| 0.0026862| 0.0268624|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |InvSimpson  |5 to 8 - Untreated   |  2.4273878| 0.0152080| 0.0760399|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |1 to 2 - 10 to 12    |  1.1048386| 0.2692295| 0.3365369|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |1 to 2 - 3 to 4      | -1.1450958| 0.2521695| 0.3602421|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |10 to 12 - 3 to 4    | -1.7659888| 0.0773977| 0.1547954|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |1 to 2 - 5 to 8      | -1.5655607| 0.1174515| 0.1957525|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |10 to 12 - 5 to 8    | -2.1803719| 0.0292299| 0.0974330|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |3 to 4 - 5 to 8      | -0.0934386| 0.9255552| 0.9255552|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |1 to 2 - Untreated   |  2.1417280| 0.0322154| 0.0805384|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |10 to 12 - Untreated |  0.2062863| 0.8365673| 0.9295192|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |3 to 4 - Untreated   |  2.2821427| 0.0224809| 0.1124046|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR    |Shannon     |5 to 8 - Untreated   |  3.0747553| 0.0021068| 0.0210675|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Observed    |<3 - >3              |  0.0774956| 0.9382293| 0.9382293|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Observed    |<3 - untreated       |  2.7685852| 0.0056300| 0.0168901|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Observed    |>3 - untreated       |  2.5342754| 0.0112680| 0.0169020|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |InvSimpson  |<3 - >3              | -1.0149679| 0.3101211| 0.3101211|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |InvSimpson  |<3 - untreated       |  2.0033455| 0.0451402| 0.0677103|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |InvSimpson  |>3 - untreated       |  3.0673747| 0.0021595| 0.0064784|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Shannon     |<3 - >3              | -0.6756323| 0.4992741| 0.4992741|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Shannon     |<3 - untreated       |  2.1417280| 0.0322154| 0.0483231|
## |YrSinceTrt_Range |Shannon     |>3 - untreated       |  2.8076768| 0.0049900| 0.0149701|
write.csv(final_dunn_table, "Results/16S/final_dunns_posthoc_table_sod_nowildfire.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s visualize alpha diversity results with rarefaction curve-treatment

sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(sod_nowildfire.ps))
otu_data <- as(otu_table(sod_nowildfire.ps), "matrix")

sample_data$Treatuntreat_EP <- factor(sample_data$Treatuntreat_EP, levels = c("Untreated", "Treated"))

colors <- ifelse(sample_data$Treatuntreat_EP == "Untreated", "blue","red")

raremax <- min(rowSums(otu_data))

pdf("Figures/16S/rarefaction_curve_trt_nowildfire.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)
rarecurve(otu_data, step = 10, sample = raremax, col = colors, cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, xlab = "Sequencing Depth", ylab = "Number of ASVs")
legend("topright", legend = levels(sample_data$Fire_Mod), col = c("blue", "red"), lty = 1, title = "Treatuntreat_EP")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s visualize alpha diversity results with rarefaction curve-year since trt

sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(sod_nowildfire.ps))
otu_data <- as(otu_table(sod_nowildfire.ps), "matrix")

colors <- ifelse(sample_data$YrSinceTrt_BR== "Untreated", "lightblue",
                 ifelse(sample_data$YrSinceTrt_BR == "1 to 2", "red", 
                        ifelse(sample_data$YrSinceTrt_BR == "3 to 4", "green", 
                               ifelse(sample_data$YrSinceTrt_BR == "5 to 8", "orange","purple"))))

raremax <- min(rowSums(otu_data))

pdf("Figures/16S/rarefaction_curve_yrsincetrt_nowildfire.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)
rarecurve(otu_data, step = 10, sample = raremax, col = colors, cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, xlab = "Sequencing Depth", ylab = "Number of ASVs")
legend("topright", legend = levels(sample_data$YrSinceTrt_BR), col = c("lightblue", "red", "green", "orange", "purple"), lty = 1, title = "YrSinceTrt_BR")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Beta diversity-PERMANOVA analyses-ASV counts transformed (rel. abundance)

set.seed(1)
mapping <- as.data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

mapping2=mapping[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range")]

ASV_tables_bray <- phyloseq::distance(strat_rel.ps, method = "bray")

df_metadata <- data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

df_metadata<-df_metadata[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR","YrSinceTrt_Range")]

pvals <- sapply(colnames(mapping2), function(variable){
  form1<-as.formula(paste("ASV_tables_bray", variable, sep="~"))
  res <- adonis2(form1, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999)
  pval <- res$`Pr(>F)`[[1]] # we take the first p value which corresponds to the variable of interest
  return(pval)
})

pval_adj <- p.adjust(pvals, method = "fdr")

save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis <- pval_adj[pval_adj < 0.05]
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis
##  Treatuntreat_EP    YrSinceTrt_BR YrSinceTrt_Range 
##           0.0237           0.0093           0.0237
print(pval_adj)
##  Treatuntreat_EP    YrSinceTrt_BR YrSinceTrt_Range 
##           0.0237           0.0093           0.0237

Compile PERMANOVA stats-main effects

set.seed(1)
res <- adonis2(ASV_tables_bray~Treatuntreat_EP+YrSinceTrt_BR, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999, by="terms")

res_data <- data.frame(res)
colnames(res_data)[colnames(res_data) == "Pr..F."] <- "pr_f"
res_data <- res_data[-c(nrow(res_data)-1, nrow(res_data)),]
res_data$FDR_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "fdr")
res_data$Bonferroni_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "bonferroni")
res_data$Factor <- rownames(res_data)
res_data <- res_data[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p")]
rownames(res_data) <- NULL

beta_disp_results <- lapply(c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR", "YrSinceTrt_Range"), function(variable) {
  res_betadisper <- betadisper(d = ASV_tables_bray, group = df_metadata[[variable]])
  perm_test_res <- permutest(res_betadisper)
  pval_betadisper <- perm_test_res$tab$`Pr(>F)`[[1]]
  
  data.frame(Factor = variable, P_Value_BetaDispersion = pval_betadisper)
})

beta_disp_results_df <- do.call(rbind, beta_disp_results)

final_results <- merge(res_data, beta_disp_results_df, by = "Factor", all.x = TRUE)

final_results <- final_results[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p", "P_Value_BetaDispersion")]

print(final_results, row.names = FALSE)
##           Factor Df  SumOfSqs         R2        F   pr_f FDR_adj_p
##  Treatuntreat_EP  1 0.3696257 0.01851453 1.569185 0.0219    0.0219
##    YrSinceTrt_BR  3 0.9858023 0.04937877 1.395020 0.0119    0.0219
##  Bonferroni_adj_p P_Value_BetaDispersion
##            0.0438                  0.020
##            0.0238                  0.199
write.csv(final_results, file = "Results/16S/PERMANOVA_with_betadisper_maineffects_nowildfire.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Compile PERMANOVA stats-interactions

set.seed(1)
res <- adonis2(ASV_tables_bray~Treatuntreat_EP*YrSinceTrt_BR, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999, by="terms")

res_data <- data.frame(res)
colnames(res_data)[colnames(res_data) == "Pr..F."] <- "pr_f"
res_data <- res_data[-c(nrow(res_data)-1, nrow(res_data)),]
res_data$FDR_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "fdr")
res_data$Bonferroni_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "bonferroni")
res_data$Factor <- rownames(res_data)
res_data <- res_data[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p")]
rownames(res_data) <- NULL

beta_disp_results <- lapply(c("Treatuntreat_EP", "YrSinceTrt_BR","YrSinceTrt_Range"), function(variable) {
  res_betadisper <- betadisper(d = ASV_tables_bray, group = df_metadata[[variable]])
  perm_test_res <- permutest(res_betadisper)
  pval_betadisper <- perm_test_res$tab$`Pr(>F)`[[1]]
  
  data.frame(Factor = variable, P_Value_BetaDispersion = pval_betadisper)
})

beta_disp_results_df <- do.call(rbind, beta_disp_results)

final_results <- merge(res_data, beta_disp_results_df, by = "Factor", all.x = TRUE)

final_results <- final_results[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p", "P_Value_BetaDispersion")]

print(final_results, row.names = FALSE)
##           Factor Df  SumOfSqs         R2        F   pr_f FDR_adj_p
##  Treatuntreat_EP  1 0.3696257 0.01851453 1.569185 0.0219    0.0219
##    YrSinceTrt_BR  3 0.9858023 0.04937877 1.395020 0.0119    0.0219
##  Bonferroni_adj_p P_Value_BetaDispersion
##            0.0438                  0.020
##            0.0238                  0.199
write.csv(final_results, file = "Results/16S/PERMANOVA_with_betadisper_interactions_nowildfire.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s now examine beta diversity through ordination plots

We’ll will examine outputs for rarefied data as well as data adjusted by relative abundance We’ll now make ordination plots on data for which we’ve calculated relative abundance

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS", 
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.1944914 
## Run 1 stress 0.1943574 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04434688  max resid 0.2769073 
## Run 2 stress 0.2127522 
## Run 3 stress 0.2069991 
## Run 4 stress 0.1862564 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04561006  max resid 0.276794 
## Run 5 stress 0.2175236 
## Run 6 stress 0.1866204 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02875016  max resid 0.1325045 
## Run 7 stress 0.2101495 
## Run 8 stress 0.1947815 
## Run 9 stress 0.189079 
## Run 10 stress 0.2219645 
## Run 11 stress 0.2089481 
## Run 12 stress 0.2044009 
## Run 13 stress 0.2053213 
## Run 14 stress 0.1919723 
## Run 15 stress 0.2064425 
## Run 16 stress 0.1894724 
## Run 17 stress 0.2134374 
## Run 18 stress 0.2065311 
## Run 19 stress 0.1874513 
## Run 20 stress 0.2107781 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     19: stress ratio > sratmax
##      1: scale factor of the gradient < sfgrmin
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         color= "Treatuntreat_EP") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  labs(color = "Factor") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))

plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

write.csv(ps_NMDS[["points"]], file="Results/16S/16S_NMDS_coordinates_persample_nowildfire.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot rel abundance-with YrSinceTrt_BR factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.1944914 
## Run 1 stress 0.1943574 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04434688  max resid 0.2769073 
## Run 2 stress 0.2127522 
## Run 3 stress 0.2069991 
## Run 4 stress 0.1862564 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04561006  max resid 0.276794 
## Run 5 stress 0.2175236 
## Run 6 stress 0.1866204 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02875016  max resid 0.1325045 
## Run 7 stress 0.2101495 
## Run 8 stress 0.1947815 
## Run 9 stress 0.189079 
## Run 10 stress 0.2219645 
## Run 11 stress 0.2089481 
## Run 12 stress 0.2044009 
## Run 13 stress 0.2053213 
## Run 14 stress 0.1919723 
## Run 15 stress 0.2064425 
## Run 16 stress 0.1894724 
## Run 17 stress 0.2134374 
## Run 18 stress 0.2065311 
## Run 19 stress 0.1874513 
## Run 20 stress 0.2107781 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     19: stress ratio > sratmax
##      1: scale factor of the gradient < sfgrmin
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "YrSinceTrt_BR") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot rel abundance-with YrSinceTrt_Range factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.1944914 
## Run 1 stress 0.1943574 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04434688  max resid 0.2769073 
## Run 2 stress 0.2127522 
## Run 3 stress 0.2069991 
## Run 4 stress 0.1862564 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04561006  max resid 0.276794 
## Run 5 stress 0.2175236 
## Run 6 stress 0.1866204 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02875016  max resid 0.1325045 
## Run 7 stress 0.2101495 
## Run 8 stress 0.1947815 
## Run 9 stress 0.189079 
## Run 10 stress 0.2219645 
## Run 11 stress 0.2089481 
## Run 12 stress 0.2044009 
## Run 13 stress 0.2053213 
## Run 14 stress 0.1919723 
## Run 15 stress 0.2064425 
## Run 16 stress 0.1894724 
## Run 17 stress 0.2134374 
## Run 18 stress 0.2065311 
## Run 19 stress 0.1874513 
## Run 20 stress 0.2107781 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     19: stress ratio > sratmax
##      1: scale factor of the gradient < sfgrmin
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "YrSinceTrt_Range") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

### Are communities more similar if geographically closeby? Some plots, like wildfire plots are close together, for example Very preliminary experiment

otu_table <- otu_table(strat_rel.ps)
sample_data <- sample_data(strat_rel.ps)
otu_table_matrix <- t(as(otu_table, "matrix"))
lon_lat <- cbind(sample_data$Longitude, sample_data$Latitude)
geo_dist <- distm(lon_lat, fun = distHaversine)
comm_dist <- vegdist(otu_table_matrix, method = "bray", na.rm = TRUE)
## Warning in vegdist(otu_table_matrix, method = "bray", na.rm = TRUE): you have empty rows: their dissimilarities may be
##                  meaningless in method "bray"
## Warning in vegdist(otu_table_matrix, method = "bray", na.rm = TRUE): missing
## values in results
comm_dist_matrix <- as.matrix(comm_dist)
sample_ids <- rownames(sample_data)
sample_ids_in_otu <- rownames(otu_table)
common_samples <- intersect(sample_ids_in_otu, sample_ids)
common_indices <- which(sample_ids %in% common_samples)
geo_dist_aligned <- geo_dist[common_indices, common_indices]
comm_dist_aligned <- comm_dist_matrix[common_indices, common_indices]


print(dim(geo_dist_aligned))
## [1] 84 84
print(dim(comm_dist_aligned))
## [1] 84 84
mantel_result <- mantel(geo_dist_aligned, comm_dist_aligned, method = "spearman", permutations = 999)
print(mantel_result)
## 
## Mantel statistic based on Spearman's rank correlation rho 
## 
## Call:
## mantel(xdis = geo_dist_aligned, ydis = comm_dist_aligned, method = "spearman",      permutations = 999) 
## 
## Mantel statistic r: -0.03681 
##       Significance: 0.758 
## 
## Upper quantiles of permutations (null model):
##    90%    95%  97.5%    99% 
## 0.0699 0.0927 0.1148 0.1356 
## Permutation: free
## Number of permutations: 999
otu_table <- otu_table(strat_rel.ps)
sample_data <- sample_data(strat_rel.ps)
otu_table_matrix <- as(otu_table, "matrix")

comm_dist <- vegdist(otu_table_matrix, method = "bray")

nmds_result <- metaMDS(comm_dist, k = 2, trymax = 100)
## Run 0 stress 0.1944914 
## Run 1 stress 0.2009595 
## Run 2 stress 0.1882025 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04544733  max resid 0.2219524 
## Run 3 stress 0.2142978 
## Run 4 stress 0.2056124 
## Run 5 stress 0.1884159 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.0185947  max resid 0.1314517 
## Run 6 stress 0.1977173 
## Run 7 stress 0.2070344 
## Run 8 stress 0.2005994 
## Run 9 stress 0.218218 
## Run 10 stress 0.1860141 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01779843  max resid 0.1140766 
## Run 11 stress 0.1883345 
## Run 12 stress 0.1903388 
## Run 13 stress 0.1975967 
## Run 14 stress 0.2166313 
## Run 15 stress 0.1870706 
## Run 16 stress 0.1864666 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.0151494  max resid 0.1319438 
## Run 17 stress 0.2101382 
## Run 18 stress 0.186668 
## Run 19 stress 0.1952315 
## Run 20 stress 0.1884025 
## Run 21 stress 0.208413 
## Run 22 stress 0.1975864 
## Run 23 stress 0.1851616 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01776733  max resid 0.1247859 
## Run 24 stress 0.2056477 
## Run 25 stress 0.210432 
## Run 26 stress 0.2123973 
## Run 27 stress 0.2080196 
## Run 28 stress 0.1962575 
## Run 29 stress 0.1866679 
## Run 30 stress 0.186668 
## Run 31 stress 0.2129201 
## Run 32 stress 0.2037802 
## Run 33 stress 0.2038303 
## Run 34 stress 0.198063 
## Run 35 stress 0.2202663 
## Run 36 stress 0.194104 
## Run 37 stress 0.1994864 
## Run 38 stress 0.2078993 
## Run 39 stress 0.1905992 
## Run 40 stress 0.2178894 
## Run 41 stress 0.1859412 
## Run 42 stress 0.2060548 
## Run 43 stress 0.2041229 
## Run 44 stress 0.2175864 
## Run 45 stress 0.2087257 
## Run 46 stress 0.206438 
## Run 47 stress 0.1948049 
## Run 48 stress 0.1885896 
## Run 49 stress 0.1935048 
## Run 50 stress 0.1896541 
## Run 51 stress 0.1997554 
## Run 52 stress 0.1942159 
## Run 53 stress 0.2158561 
## Run 54 stress 0.1993045 
## Run 55 stress 0.1851617 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 5.568393e-05  max resid 0.0002834094 
## ... Similar to previous best
## *** Best solution repeated 1 times
nmds_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds_result))
nmds_scores$SampleID <- rownames(nmds_scores)

sample_ids <- rownames(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))  # Correct sample IDs
sample_coords <- data.frame(SampleID = sample_ids,
                             Longitude = sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Longitude,
                             Latitude = sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Latitude, 
                             Treatuntreat=sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Treatuntreat_EP,
                             Fire_Mod=sample_data(strat_rel.ps)$Fire_Mod
                            )

nmds_scores <- merge(nmds_scores, sample_coords, by = "SampleID")

nmds_plot <- ggplot(nmds_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Latitude, size = Longitude), alpha = 0.7) +  # Use size for Longitude
  labs(title = "NMDS Plot with Latitude and Longitude",
       x = "NMDS Dimension 1",
       y = "NMDS Dimension 2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +  
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6))  

print(nmds_plot)

nmds_plot <- ggplot(nmds_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Latitude, size = Longitude), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Fire_Mod) +
  labs(title = "NMDS Plot with Latitude, Longitude, and Treatment",
       x = "NMDS Dimension 1",
       y = "NMDS Dimension 2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6))

print(nmds_plot)
